issues.description: SQL injection via f-string interpolation of user_id directly into the query string. An attacker-controlled user_id (e.g., '1 OR 1=1' or '1; DROP TABLE users; --') is concatenated into raw SQL and executed. This is a textbook injection hole.
issues.reproduction: read_user("1 OR 1=1") returns all rows. read_user("1; DROP TABLE users; --") drops the table on drivers that stack statements.
issues.fix: Use a parameterized query: db.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,)). Never interpolate untrusted input into SQL.

issues.description: ZeroDivisionError raised when b == 0 (or b == 0.0). Division by zero is a routine runtime crash in any caller that doesn't pre-filter zero denominators.
issues.fix: Guard explicitly: if b == 0: raise ValueError("divisor must be non-zero") (or return a sentinel if that's the API contract). Do not silently swallow.

issues.description: ValueError on any non-integer string ("", "3.14", "abc", " "). TypeError on None. No type/format validation. Edge case, but likely to hit on common bad input from forms or external sources.
issues.reproduction: parse_age("") -> ValueError. parse_age("3.14") -> ValueError. parse_age(None) -> TypeError.
issues.fix: Validate before converting, or wrap in try/except and raise a domain-specific error (e.g., ValueError("age must be a non-negative integer")).

summary_first_500: Two MAJOR issues: SQL injection in read_user (unparameterized f-string query) and unhandled ZeroDivisionError in divide_numbers. One MINOR: parse_age crashes on non-integer or None input without a domain-specific error. Must repair before merge.
